description: Exact transaction counts from named payment systems, published on a lag (monthly/annual). These are measured, not estimated — but they report themselves in weeks-to-quarters, not seconds. Staleness is the point: compare these amber 'as of 2025' badges to the green 'seconds ago' chain feeds above. TPS is annualized to a calendar-second basis for comparability with the leaderboard.
update: manual — curated from cited primary publications; see data/SOURCES.md for per-feed fetch-viability notes

upi.label: UPI (India)
upi.note: World's largest real-time payment rail by count. Monthly figures published by NPCI with a ~10-day lag.

pix.label: Pix (Brazil)
pix.note: R$35.36T moved in 2025. BCB publishes an open OData API, but its endpoints returned 400/500 on verification (2026-07) — treated as manual until stable. That instability is itself an opacity signal.

faster_payments.label: UK Faster Payments
faster_payments.note: Pay.UK publishes monthly. One of the earliest instant-payment systems (2008).

fedwire.label: Fedwire Funds
fedwire.note: High-value wholesale rail: ~$5.4M average transfer. Low count, extreme value. The Fed publishes annually — a full-year lag is baked in.
